🌅 Discover Your Neighborhood

💡
How this works
Answer 24 questions honestly — there are no right or wrong answers. Choose what feels most like you, not what you think sounds good. Your results will identify which of 7 career neighborhoods best matches your strengths and natural tendencies.

24 questions reveal which of 6 career neighborhoods matches your strengths. No right or wrong answers.

Stage 2: Find your sub-neighborhood

Complete Stage 1 first to unlock Stage 2.

🔍
Narrowing your path
Stage 2 takes your neighborhood result and drills deeper. 14 questions will identify your specific sub-neighborhood — the cluster of careers that most closely matches how you like to work. This unlocks your personalized career list.

Career Explorer

Complete the Stage 2 assessment to unlock your personalized career matches from over 1,000 BLS-backed careers.

Your career matches

💼
How to use Career Explorer
Search by job title or keyword (try "doctor", "engineer", "pilot").
Click any card to see salary, growth rate, and a full education pathway.
☆ Save careers that interest you — they appear on your Profile with a step-by-step plan.
Scope buttons let you browse your sub-neighborhood, full neighborhood, or all 1,261 careers.

Education Roadmap

Your personalized path from school to career — degree options, community college, trade programs, and scholarships.

🎓
Compare education pathways
Click any pathway card to see a detailed breakdown of schools, costs, timelines, and financial aid options. Use the Financial Aid tab to calculate your estimated net cost after grants and scholarships.
🚀 Full personalization coming soon
🎓
4-Year University
Best for careers requiring professional licensure or graduate school.
Average cost: $25,000–$55,000/yr
Timeline: 4 years
Pell Grant eligible up to $7,395/yr
🏫
Community College
Start here, transfer later. Save $40,000+ on the first two years.
Average cost: $3,500–$6,000/yr
Timeline: 2 years + transfer
Often fully covered by FAFSA grants
🔧
Trade / Vocational
Fastest path to a skilled, well-paying career. Apprenticeships pay while you learn.
Average cost: $5,000–$15,000 total
Timeline: 6 months – 2 years
Apprenticeships pay $18–$30/hr
💻
Bootcamp / Certification
Focused, intensive programs in tech, data, healthcare, and more.
Average cost: $5,000–$20,000 total
Timeline: 3–12 months
Income-sharing options available
Your timeline

Every institution — click any category to browse, compare costs, scholarships, grants, and majors.

🌎 Workforce Intelligence

Explore county workforce data — select from the dropdown or click a city on the map for a full Workforce Intelligence Dashboard.

🗺️
How to explore workforce data
County dropdown — select any of Alabama's 66 counties for a full workforce dashboard with top employers, in-demand jobs, and nearest colleges.
City map — click any dot on the map or city card on the right for a city-level profile.
Jefferson County (Birmingham) loads instantly. All others load in 5–10 seconds.

🌎 Alabama’s Career Platform
LaunchPoint Navigator
A complete career exploration and planning platform for Alabama students — from discovering your strengths to mapping your education pathway, understanding your finances, and exploring local workforce opportunities.
1,261
Real Careers
66
AL Counties
7
Neighborhoods
7
Fin Lit Modules
🌍 How It Works
1
🌅 Discover Your Neighborhood
Take 24 questions to reveal which of 7 career neighborhoods matches your natural strengths and working style. No right or wrong answers.
2
🔎 Refine Your Focus
14 follow-up questions narrow you into a specific sub-neighborhood — the precise cluster of careers that fits how you think and work.
3
💼 Explore Real Careers
Browse 1,261 careers with real BLS salary and growth data. Search by keyword, save favorites, and get a step-by-step education pathway for each one.
4
📚 Build Financial Literacy
Six interactive modules covering income, budgeting, debt, college costs, and smart money decisions — with a quiz after each one.
5
🎓 Plan Your Education
Compare degree pathways, community college, trade programs, and scholarships. Calculate your actual cost after financial aid.
6
💵 Simulate Your Budget
Practice living on a career salary — see exactly how income, housing, transportation, and savings interact across different life scenarios.
7
🌎 Explore Your Workforce
Select any of Alabama’s 66 counties for a full Workforce Intelligence Dashboard — top employers, in-demand jobs, salary data, and nearest colleges.
8
★ Your Career Plan
Everything comes together on My Profile — your score, saved careers with education pathways, financial aid plan, and next steps all in one place.
🏠 The 7 Career Neighborhoods
🎨
Creator / Storyteller
Visual arts, media, writing, performance
🔨
Builder / Maker
Construction, trades, engineering, manufacturing
📈
Business / Leader
Entrepreneurship, finance, sales, operations
❤️
Helper / Advocate
Healthcare, education, social work, nonprofit
🔎
Analyst / Investigator
Research, data, law, policy, compliance
💻
Technology / Innovator
Software, cybersecurity, AI, IT systems
🍽️
Military / Service
Army, Navy, Air Force, Space Force, intel
🤔 Who Is This For?

LaunchPoint Navigator is designed for students in grades 6–12 who are starting to think about their future. It works equally well for a 7th grader just beginning to explore, a 10th grader choosing classes, or a 12th grader making college and career decisions. All content is grade-banded — younger students see age-appropriate language and scenarios automatically. The platform is also used by parents, school counselors, and credit unions as a structured career exploration tool for the young people they serve.

📊 The Data Behind It

Every career’s salary and growth rate comes from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Occupational Outlook Handbook 2023–2024. Physician specialty salaries reflect MGMA and Medscape compensation reports. Military pay reflects DoD Basic Pay scales. Alabama county workforce data is sourced from the Alabama Department of Labor and U.S. Census Bureau. All data is reviewed and updated annually.

LaunchPoint Navigator
Built for Alabama students — powered by real data.
Choose Your Grade
The questions and content adjust to match your grade level. Pick the one closest to yours.
